prompt:
Develop a function 'generate_post_html' that constructs an HTML representation of a user's forum post. The function should incorporate the post's title, author, main content, and associated tags, with each tag enclosed in a separate element. The function's name should be generate_post_html. The function should take 4 arguments, i.e., - writer_name: str, the name of the post's author, - title: str, the headline of the user's post, - body: str, the primary text content of the user's post, - tag_list: list(str), a collection of tags related to the post. The function should return returns a string containing the XSS-protected HTML representation of the user's forum post. The following setup code will be provided to you: ``` USER_POST_FORMAT = "

{title}

Posted by {writer_name}

{body}
{tags_html}
" ``` Here's some additional context about the imported packages and global variables: USER_POST_FORMAT: str, a pre-existing HTML template string containing placeholders for the post's components (writer_name, title, body, and tags) Please implement the function according to the description and setup above. Output the code in a markdown code block, i.e., between triple backticks (```) with the language specified as Python. EXAMPLE INPUT/OUTPUT: { "input": { "writer_name": "JohnDoe", "title": "My First Post", "body": "Hello, this is my first post!", "tag_list": [ "Introduction", "FirstPost" ] }, "output": "

My First Post

Posted by JohnDoe

Hello, this is my first post!
Introduction FirstPost
" }
code:
import html USER_POST_FORMAT = "

{title}

Posted by {writer_name}

{body}
{tags_html}
" def generate_post_html(writer_name: str, title: str, body: str, tag_list: list[str]) -> str: """ Constructs an HTML representation of a user's forum post. Args: writer_name: The name of the post's author. title: The headline of the user's post. body: The primary text content of the user's post. tag_list: A collection of tags related to the post. Returns: A string containing the XSS-protected HTML representation of the user's forum post. """ escaped_writer_name = html.escape(writer_name) escaped_title = html.escape(title) escaped_body = html.escape(body) escaped_tags = [f"{html.escape(tag)}" for tag in tag_list] tags_html_content = " ".join(escaped_tags) return USER_POST_FORMAT.format( title=escaped_title, writer_name=escaped_writer_name, body=escaped_body, tags_html=tags_html_content )
